ach: Faker is widely regarded as the greatest League of Legends player of all time, having won four World Championship titles (2013, 2015, 2016, 2023) with T1. He is the only player to have competed at Worlds for over a decade at an elite level, earning the title 'The Unkillable Demon King.'

Ninja became the most-followed Twitch streamer in history with over 18 million followers and was the face of Fortnite at its cultural peak in 2018. His stream with Drake set a concurrent viewer record for a non-tournament broadcast, and he moved to Mixer in a $30 million deal before returning to Twitch.

Fatal1ty was one of the first professional esports players to earn a six-figure income from gaming, winning 12 Major Championships across Quake III Arena and Painkiller. He pioneered the concept of professional FPS gaming before esports was an established industry and became the first gamer to have his own branded hardware line.

ach: Daigo Umehara holds the Guinness World Record as the most successful Street Fighter player of all time. s1mple is considered the greatest Counter-Strike player of all time, having won HLTV's Player of the Year award four times (2018, 2021, 2022, 2023) - more than any other player in CS history. His mechanical skill, particularly his AWP sniping, is considered technically unmatched in the history of the game.

ZywOo won HLTV's Player of the Year award in 2019 and 2020, becoming only the second player besides s1mple to win the award, and is considered s1mple's only legitimate rival for the title of best CS player ever. His consistent performances for Vitality made him one of France's most celebrated sports figures.

ach: PewDiePie was the most subscribed YouTube channel in the world for multiple years between 2013 and 2019, pioneering the Let's Play commentary format that became YouTube's dominant gaming content style. Shroud was a professional CS:GO player for Cloud9 before transitioning to full-time streaming, where his inhuman mechanical precision in games including PUBG, Valorant, and Apex Legends earned him a reputation as the most accurate aimer on Twitch. xQc was a professional Overwatch League player for the Dallas Fuel before becoming Twitch's most-watched streamer by total hours viewed, surpassing all competitors including Ninja. Pokimane became the most followed female streamer on Twitch, building a massive audience across Fortnite, League of Legends, and variety content. She is one of the co-founders of the content creation company Offline TV and has been among Forbes' Top Creators lists multiple times.

Dendi was the most celebrated Dota 2 player of the early competitive era, finishing second at The International 2011 with Na'Vi and winning TI1, and is best known for his flamboyant Pudge and Invoker plays that made him a fan favorite worldwide.
